title: Distributed Governance Research: A Functional Architecture for Selective Institutional Distribution (Working Paper v1.13)
description: Complete research corpus, working paper, and code proposing a functional architecture for distributed public project governance: citizens allocate a mandate-recorded share of an existing public budget across verifiable projects under strict role separation, milestone-gated conditional disbursement, evidential contracts, and role-based reputation — distributed allocation within mandate-bound, visible, versioned, and contestable planning scopes. Includes formal mechanism-design propositions (incentive compatibility of milestone disbursement; collusion-proofness of protocolized fiscalization), a reproducible 10,000-agent simulation of eight experiments (six with pre-registered designs; the one genuinely confirmatory pre-registered test is the later symmetry gate), plus that later pre-registered symmetric selection-only stress test (NO-GO) that retires the earlier compound value-per-budget multiplier as a calibrated effect (the distributed selection advantage is real but small -- a conditional, stylized-model output, not a calibrated real-world effect and not a validated Core v0 implementation; the controlling specification is research/claim-and-estimand-contract.md), and a documented adversarial review record of forty-three attack briefs across five rounds with paired defenses and accepted resolutions, propagated through the architecture corpus except the manuscript-review round's D037-D040, whose propagation is tracked. The manuscript is drafts/paper.md (Spanish translation drafts/paper.es.md); the accessible companion is docs/101_CORE_V0_MINIMAL_PUBLISHABLE_MODEL.md.
notes: Single record covering the corpus, the working paper, and code. All text and research content (corpus + manuscript) is CC BY 4.0; code (scripts/) is MIT. CC BY attribution is required for text; the MIT notice must be retained for code. See LICENSE.md.
